items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Fenrico, stop! The Threnodian is using you! You are the Harbinger of the Dark Tide!

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Absurd! I stand against it. How could I ever be its puppet?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: O Sentinel! Return the souls in darkness to this world! Let all wills be made one!

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The Gem… is fleeing into the Dark Tide?

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: My predecessors... What you taught... I...

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: "The path born of injustice must be fortified with further transgressions…"

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Hah, no, even with the body perished, hope and salvation shall endure.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Righteous One, please, save Rinascita's souls from the Paradise...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Your plan won't work.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Says the one who hasn't witnessed the years, the failures, the countless attempts I've made. What gives you the audacity to pass such judgment?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You've already tried everything! Can't you see? The path you're on only leads here, nowhere beyond.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Among thousands of paths, surely one will lead me back to them.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: And even if I'm wrong... I fear not starting over. I've done it time and time again.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What meaning is there in chasing something that's neither realistic nor attainable?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I know they're there.

options.text: I saw them in the Beyond.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Those fading Reverberations did not, and cannot, return to life. No matter how long you hold onto their frequencies, the result will be the same.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The villagers are dead. What you've created are Tacet Discords. If you continue turning the entire town into that... you're not creating a paradise. You're creating a hell.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: "The villagers are dead..."

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: So, you saw them in their human form.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Then how can you claim my goal is unattainable?!

options.text: Your goal is the one the Fractsidus gave you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's what they forced on you. And the Triss you saw? Fleeting, unstable, an illusion. And yet, you still believe everything they say can become real?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The Fractsidus showed me a way forward.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Even if they have their own motives, even if we're only using each other... I care not.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: If they can help me take this step, then another step may follow. And another. No matter how steep the price, I'll pay it, step by step, until I reach the end I seek.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Love? Sincerity? Compassion? Trust?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: What you and your cronies hold dear is but a jest to the Fractsidus. No bond lasts forever. Without true loyalties, there can be no betrayal, no deceit.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I no longer buy into such illusions.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: After all, even if you believe with all your heart, even if you wait patiently at a crossroads...

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The one you're waiting for may have long since forgotten you. Forgotten the promise they swore to keep.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I waited. For a long time.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I won't wait anymore.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: What was once an unforgettable encounter between us can now be the thread that weaves a paradise for "them."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You've gone too far.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Heh, you're one to talk. How amusing.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You, with your long life and its endless possibilities, still choosing to start over, even at the cost of all your memories?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The irreplaceable bonds, the emotions that make us human, are the brightest lights and most precious treasures in our fragile existence.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: What madness, what cruelty, what impossible goal would drive you to cast them all aside?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You've gone further than I ever could.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Perhaps you believe more than I do that only in endless time can true, eternal perfection arise. That only then will a brave new world arrive.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: We walk different paths, yet in the end, we are both driven by the same madness. Why argue who's fallen further?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The only difference is that... you've forgotten it all.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You never understood the true value of human life.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Nor have you stepped beyond the boundary that death has placed upon the human soul.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Hehe...

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: So, this is our finale.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I'm sorry.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I've come home.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Fenrico spoke of a... Paradise. What does it mean?)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (And "save Rinascita's souls"... Was he pointing to the source of the anomaly in Rinascita?)

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I heard curses... not loud, but deep... I deserve them.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Soon, It will gather enough strength to herd the people and drown Rinascita beneath the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: The Gem isn't the true heart of the problem. The false Journeying Paradise has created an illusion of everlasting peace, hiding the real source of the catastrophe.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I will remain here to stem the Dark Tide's spread.

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: Now, I can sense it clearly. The fluctuations from the Paradise are not in Ragunna, but in Septimont...

items.speaker: Fenrico
items.text: I cannot reach that far. I must rely on you to go, find, and solve the crisis there. Only then can those who abandoned their faith save their homeland.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Phew, that was exhausting...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I can't believe it! That gate sent me to some lab so, so far away!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Finally made it back... So? What happened after that? Did we win?

options.text: The Fractsidus got away with the Gem.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: What? What should we do now? Let's track them down!

options.text: Not now. There's something more important.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Huh? What's the bigger deal?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We were after the Gem to find the source of the anomaly in Rinascita.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Now we have a better lead—the Journeying Paradise.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That might also explain why the Fractsidus took the Gem and what they're really after.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We'll head to Septimont to uncover the truth.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Maybe it's time we spoke with the Ephor.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh... Alright. Are we heading back now?

options.text: We still have to tie up loose ends here.

items.text: "Once I dreamt that we were dear to each other."
"I woke to find that we were strangers."

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The Gem's power grows.

items.speaker: Fenrico & Leviathan
items.text: Salvation, Ascension, and Unity. This will be the one true path.
